raw_consumer_statement: I received a text message on XX/XX/XXXX alerting me that my credit card was charged {$1700.00} XXXX Dollars on XX/XX/XXXX while on holiday vacation in XXXX XXXX. I immediately called the number on the back of the card and reported this unauthorized charge. I was told on a recorded line that I could continue to use my card and make a formal dispute when I returned to my home in XXXX, Arizona XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX I received a letter from Barclays that the investigation determined that the charge was a valid charge and I was responsible for the payment. The provisional credit was reversed and the charge in now on my card as of XX/XX/XXXX. They mentioned that since chip technology was used and can not be counterfeited the charge is valid. My dispute with Barclays is that it was a possible that fraudulent use of my card was made at the restaurant I had dinner since the card was provided for payment and paid with MXN paseos. The card was out of my possession when the waitress left with my card to make the transaction, and my card was returned to me. The restaurant was contacted, and they have no record of this fraudulent charge and I have misplaced my receipt. Barclays refused to credit my account. New card and number change has been sent to me.
